Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC154 describes decomposing a requirement and sending a decompose event and temporary links, but it does not mention use of a 3rd party tool (DOORS) or any interaction with such a tool.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC155 describes refining a requirement and sending a refine event with temporary links; there is no mention of DOORS or any 3rd party RM tool interaction.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC156 describes modifying a requirement and sending a modify event; it does not indicate that the requirement is managed in DOORS or another 3rd party tool.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC158 covers merging requirements and related link reassignment, but it does not reference DOORS or other 3rd party RM tool usage.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: The test explicitly states the system polls DOORS to obtain requirement-related quantitative information and uses that information to construct and send composite event messages. This demonstrates integration with DOORS as the 3rd party RM tool named in the requirement.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC141 covers establishing a trace between a UML artifact and a requirement by a registered subscriber. It addresses trace functionality, not management of non-requirement artifacts in third-party tools.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC142 describes a non-registered subscriber attempting to establish a trace; focuses on trace establishment authorization and expected postcondition that no trace is established. No mention of third-party tools (Rational Rose, Clear case) or management of non-requirement artifacts.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC154 addresses decomposing a requirement and related event messages and temporary links. No mention of third-party tool management for non-requirement artifacts.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC155 concerns refining a requirement and sending refine events with temporary links. It does not reference or validate management of non-requirement artifacts in external tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC157 covers deleting a requirement and resulting link/postcondition behaviors. This concerns requirement lifecycle and link states, not management of non-requirement artifacts in third-party tools.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC162 describes event server constructing composite event messages by polling DOORS and sending customized messages to subscribers. It references DOORS (a requirements tool) but does not describe managing non-requirement artifacts in third-party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case; therefore it does not verify RQ101.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC165 concerns a user processing event messages and updating links; it addresses link selection and event processing behavior, not integration with or management in external tools like Rational Rose or Clear case.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test asserts the positive/happy-path: a registered subscriber successfully establishes a trace. It demonstrates allowance but does not by itself show that non-registered subscribers are prevented.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test explicitly asserts the negative/deny behavior required by RQ102 (non-registered subscriber attempts to establish a trace and no trace is established). It covers the prohibition side but alone does not exercise the positive allowance.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test verifies that a subscriber can register with a subscriber manager (registration flow). While registration is a prerequisite for RQ102, the test does not itself assert that only registered subscribers may establish traces.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test concerns a subscriber failing to register and being removed. It does not assert behavior about trace establishment attempts by non-registered vs registered subscribers.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test verifies subscriber manager registration with an event server and does not assert trace-establishment permission enforcement for subscribers.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test concerns event notification handling for registered subscribers and does not assert trace-establishment permission enforcement.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: TC142 concerns actions by a non-registered subscriber (attempting to establish a trace) and asserts that no trace is established; it does not verify successful registration behavior required by RQ103.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: TC143 explicitly exercises the registration action and asserts the postcondition that the subscriber is registered under the subscriber manager, directly satisfying the requirement's behavior.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: TC144 addresses the registration process but covers a failure/negative path (subscriber does not complete registration). It is topically related but does not assert a successful registration, so it does not fully verify the requirement's positive behavior.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: TC146 exercises Subscriber Manager registration with an event server; it does not relate to a user registering as a subscriber and contains no assertions about subscriber registration.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: TC147 concerns real-time event publication and subscription state; it does not contain assertions about a user registering as a subscriber.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: TC149 concerns event notification receipt and logging for subscriber managers; it does not assert or verify that a user registers as a subscriber.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: The test step explicitly performs registration with a subscriber manager and the postcondition asserts the subscriber is registered under that manager, directly satisfying the requirement's mandated behavior.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test describes a failure to register (subscriber exits registration and is removed). It does not assert that a subscriber successfully registers under a subscriber manager, so it does not verify the positive requirement.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test verifies subscriber manager registration with an event server (manager registers IP/port/name). It does not address subscriber registration under a subscriber manager.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test concerns event publication and assumes subscriber manager subscriptions; it does not assert that a subscriber registers under a subscriber manager.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test is about non-speculative event notifications being received and logged; it does not exercise or assert subscriber registration under a manager.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test covers reception and processing of event notifications by a subscriber manager and assumes registered subscribers exist, but it does not assert the subscriber registration action itself.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: This test explicitly asserts that a subscriber establishes a trace between a UML artifact and a requirement and that a trace exists as a postcondition, which directly addresses the requirement's goal to establish traces between requirements and artifacts. However, the test description does not explicitly state that the trace is created via a subscription 'from an artifact to the relevant requirement' (direction/mechanism). Therefore the core behavior is covered but the specific subscription-from-artifact semantics is not confirmed.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: This negative test verifies that an unregistered subscriber cannot establish a trace between an artifact and a requirement, exercising an error/authorization path related to the trace-establishment workflow. It supports the requirement by testing a constraint on who can create traces, but it does not demonstrate the prescribed subscription mechanism 'from an artifact to the requirement' explicitly.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: This test demonstrates a subscription-based notification mechanism (a subscriber manager subscribed to a requirement and receiving events). It shows subscription behavior related to requirements, but the subscriber is a manager (external) rather than an artifact subscribing to a requirement; therefore it does not fully demonstrate the specific artifact-to-requirement subscription the requirement mandates.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: This test verifies creating refinement links between requirements (requirement-to-requirement linkage). It does not address creating traces between requirements and other types of artifacts nor the artifact->requirement subscription mechanism required by the requirement.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: This test exercise concerns the use of existing requirement-to-artifact links when composing event messages and assumes links already exist. It does not assert or verify creation of traces/subscriptions (especially not subscriptions from an artifact to a requirement). Thus it is topically related but provides no direct verification of the requirement.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test asserts that a subscriber (an actor) is registered under a subscriber manager, which is topically related to assigning control to a manager. It does not mention artifacts or that artifacts are placed under control of a subscriber manager, nor does it assert that every artifact is assigned.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This negative test concerns subscriber registration failure and removal. It does not assert any behavior about artifacts being placed under subscriber manager control.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test concerns subscriber manager registration with the event server. It does not assert that artifacts are assigned to a subscriber manager.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test shows a subscriber manager subscribing to requirements and receiving notifications about requirement changes. It indicates subscriber managers can be associated with requirements (which may be artifacts) but does not explicitly assert that each artifact is placed under control of a subscriber manager or that all artifacts are assigned.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test explicitly references requirements linked to the same artifact and sending customized messages to subscribers; it demonstrates artifact-level linkage to requirement notifications. However it does not assert that each artifact is placed under the control of a subscriber manager or that assignment/enforcement for every artifact exists.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test verifies event ordering enforcement for a subscriber manager; it does not address artifacts being placed under a subscriber manager's control.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: This test covers subscriber registration with a manager, not deletion of subscriptions.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: This test concerns subscriber registration failure/removal, not user-initiated deletion of subscriptions.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: The test explicitly exercises a user deleting a subscription and asserts as a postcondition that the subscription has been deleted, directly satisfying the requirement's core behaviour.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: This test exercises event publication to subscribers, not deletion of subscriptions by a user.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: This test covers deleting a requirement (setting it obsolete and related link handling), not deleting subscriptions; it does not verify the subscription-deletion requirement.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: TC143 describes a subscriber registering with a subscriber manager (subscriber->manager), not the subscriber manager registering with the eventserver; it does not verify RQ108.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: TC146 explicitly describes the subscriber manager registering its IP, port and name with the event server and states the postcondition that the manager is registered with the event server, which directly satisfies the requirement that the subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: TC148 describes a subscriber manager coming online and downloading missed events from the event server. It implies connectivity but does not assert that the manager registers itself with the eventserver, so it does not provide direct evidence for RQ108.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: TC149 describes the subscriber manager receiving a non-speculative event notification and lists the precondition "The subscriber manager has registered subscribers." It does not state or assert that the subscriber manager registers itself with the eventserver; therefore it does not verify RQ108.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: TC150 describes receipt/processing of a speculative event notification and uses the precondition "The subscriber manager has registered subscribers." It does not assert that the subscriber manager registers itself with the eventserver, so it does not verify RQ108.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: TC161 describes how the event server notifies subscriber managers (identifying and sending to IP/port) but does not include any assertion that a subscriber manager performed registration with the eventserver; thus it does not verify RQ108.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: This test explicitly states the subscriber manager is online and that an event notification is sent to the relevant subscriber manager (server → subscriber manager), which aligns with the push model and real-time publication intent. However, the test does not assert timing bounds, nor does it explicitly verify that the notification was server-initiated (as opposed to being pulled) or that delivery occurred within a real-time threshold; therefore confidence is partial.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: This test describes the subscriber manager being offline and then downloading missed events when coming online. That exercise validates a pull/download-on-reconnect behavior, not the push-when-online requirement, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: This test asserts that the server sends an event notification and that the subscriber manager receives and logs it, which supports the push delivery behavior. It lacks explicit online precondition wording and does not assert real-time delivery timing or server-initiated connection semantics, so confidence is partial.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: Similar to TC149: the test documents sending and receipt of notifications and subsequent processing, supporting push-model delivery. It does not include an explicit online precondition or timing/real-time assertions; therefore coverage is partial.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: This test explicitly describes the event server constructing and sending notifications to subscriber managers (server-initiated send) and states that messages are received. It also includes IP/port identification which supports direct push targeting. While it does not quantify timing with specific thresholds, it clearly exercises the push delivery happy path; because it includes both send and confirmed receipt language, confidence is assessed as full for core push behavior (though timing boundaries are still not measured).

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: TC146 verifies registration of the subscriber manager with the event server; registration is related infrastructure but does not assert the pull model retrieval of outstanding messages on first startup.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: TC147 describes real-time publication to online subscriber managers; it addresses push/real-time delivery, not the pull-on-startup retrieval of outstanding messages required by RQ110.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: The test explicitly sets the subscriber manager offline with outstanding event(s) on the event server, then brings the manager online and asserts that it successfully downloaded the relevant event notifications. This directly exercises the 'on first come online, pull outstanding messages' behavior required by RQ110.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: TC149 focuses on receiving non-speculative notifications and storing them in a local event log; it does not verify the startup pull retrieval of outstanding messages from the eventserver when the subscriber manager first comes online.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: TC150 describes receiving speculative event notifications while the subscriber manager is already registered; it does not exercise or assert initial startup pull behavior for outstanding messages from the event server.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: TC164 concerns processing order of already-received messages and does not address retrieving outstanding messages on startup or implementing a pull model when the subscriber manager first comes online.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: The test indicates an event notification is sent to the relevant subscriber manager when a requirement changes and the manager is online. However, it does not explicitly assert that the subscriber manager actually receives, stores, or processes the notification (no explicit receive/assertion step or postcondition). This provides partial evidence (send path) but lacks an explicit verification of receipt/handling.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: This test demonstrates the subscriber manager receiving (downloading) stored event notifications when coming online, which is a covered scenario of receiving notifications on behalf of managed artifacts (missed/delayed delivery path).

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: The test explicitly states that an event notification is sent to the subscriber manager for a registered subscriber, that the relevant subscriber manager receives the notification, and that the message is stored. This directly demonstrates receipt by the subscriber manager on behalf of a registered subscriber and verifies storage, covering the core requirement behavior.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: The test explicitly shows a notification being sent to a subscriber manager for a registered subscriber and the manager receiving and processing it. This verifies receipt and handling on behalf of a managed subscriber (artifact), satisfying the requirement's core behavior.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: The test describes the event server sending notifications to each relevant subscriber manager and explicitly states the event message is received by each relevant subscriber manager on behalf of its managed subscribers. This directly matches the requirement.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: This test focuses on display ordering of events (presentation-layer behavior). It does not contain an assertion that the subscriber manager receives notifications or that receipt occurs on behalf of managed artifacts; it only assumes messages have been received for the purpose of ordering display. Therefore it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This test demonstrates the successful establishment of a trace between an artifact and a requirement (happy path). It shows trace creation exists but does not explicitly attribute trace storage/management responsibilities to the subscriber manager nor exercises multiplicity (one or more artifacts) or other management operations (query/update/delete), so coverage is partial.

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This test verifies that a non-registered subscriber cannot establish a trace (negative path). That implies the subscriber manager enforces some control over trace establishment, which is related to 'managing traces'. However, it does not explicitly show the subscriber manager storing, associating, or managing traces for artifacts or handling multiple artifacts, so coverage is partial.

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This test verifies subscriber registration with the subscriber manager, a prerequisite for controlled trace management. It provides indirect evidence that the manager maintains subscriber state but does not directly verify trace management functions (storing/associating/managing traces or handling multiple artifacts).

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This test concerns the subscriber manager registering with an event server (IP/port/name). It does not assert any behavior related to trace management for artifacts.

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This test addresses receiving and storing event notifications in an event log. It does not assert trace creation, association, storage, or management for artifacts.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: This test documents subscriber manager behavior for non-speculative (non-speculative likely corresponding to evolutionary) events, including a concrete postcondition (store in local event log). That demonstrates different handling for a non-speculative class, providing partial evidence the subscriber manager treats event types differently. It still lacks an explicit assertion that the subscriber manager performs the classification step on receipt.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: This test targets the subscriber manager receiving a speculative event and specifies a distinct postcondition (processing per predefined protocols). It shows subscriber manager behavior for speculative events, which semantically supports differentiation. However, it does not explicitly assert that the subscriber manager classifies the message as speculative versus evolutionary at receipt time nor show the branching decision logic; therefore coverage is partial.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: This test explicitly verifies that the event server categorizes events as speculative or evolutionary. However, it demonstrates server-side classification, not classification performed by the subscriber manager upon receipt, so it does not satisfy the requirement that the subscriber manager itself differentiate on receipt.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: This test verifies that an evolutionary event is propagated by the event server to subscriber managers, and that subscriber managers receive the message. It does not assert that the subscriber manager itself differentiates between speculative and evolutionary events upon receipt; the behavior asserted is delivery/notification only.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: This test concerns event server behavior for speculative events and construction of composite messages. It does not exercise or assert subscriber manager classification behavior upon receipt.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: This test (TC146) concerns registration of the subscriber manager with the event server and does not assert any storing of evolutionary event information in an eventlog.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: This test (TC148) verifies that the subscriber manager downloads missed event notifications when coming online but does not explicitly assert that those downloaded notifications are stored in the subscriber manager's eventlog. It is semantically related but lacks an explicit storage assertion.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: This test (TC149) explicitly states that the subscriber manager stores the event notification message in the local event log for non-speculative events. Non-speculative events are equivalent to evolutionary events per the test corpus categorization, directly satisfying the requirement's core behavior.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: This test (TC150) addresses speculative events and processing protocols; it does not assert storage in an eventlog, and speculative events are not the evolutionary events targeted by the requirement.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: This test (TC159) shows the event server storing events in its own event log and categorizing events. It does not assert that the subscriber manager stores evolutionary events in its local eventlog.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: This test (TC161) concerns notifying subscriber managers when an evolutionary event occurs and only asserts receipt by subscriber managers; it does not assert that the subscriber manager stores the event in an eventlog.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC150 verifies subscriber manager handling of speculative event notifications; it addresses event receipt/processing but does not verify the user's ability to construct speculative queries with a GUI.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: The test step explicitly describes the user constructing a speculative query using a GUI tool (direct match to requirement). The postcondition verifies the outcome (speculative event message sent/received), demonstrating end-to-end behavior for the GUI action.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC154 describes decomposing a requirement (decompose event). It does not assert GUI-based speculative query construction.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC155 describes refining a requirement (specify refine event, add requirement). This is not about constructing speculative queries via a GUI.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC156 describes modifying a requirement (specify modify event). It does not address user construction of speculative queries using a GUI.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC162 describes server-side behavior after receipt of a speculative event message (constructing composite event messages). It does not assert that a user constructed the speculative query via a GUI, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC165 concerns the user processing event messages (examining and applying them). It does not assert creation of speculative queries via a GUI, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: TC149 is explicitly about a non-speculative event and its postcondition is storage in the local event log. This does not verify processing of speculative events nor any immediacy guarantee.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: TC150 explicitly exercises the subscriber manager receiving a speculative event and its postcondition states the event notification is processed. However, the test does not assert timing or the word 'immediately' (no latency, ordering vs other events, or prioritization checks). Therefore it addresses the core processing behavior but lacks evidence for the 'immediately' requirement.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: TC151 covers the user constructing a speculative query that results in a speculative event message being received by the event server; it does not exercise the subscriber manager or verify immediate processing of speculative events.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: TC162 exercises event server behavior in constructing composite messages for a speculative event and sending to subscribers, but does not assert how the subscriber manager processes those speculative events or that processing happens immediately.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: TC164 addresses ordering and user attempts to process events out of order; it does not exercise speculative events being immediately processed by the subscriber manager.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: This registration test verifies subscriber registration but has no assertions about model execution or use of speculative values by the subscriber manager.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: This test deals with non-speculative events and storing the notification in a log. It does not assert any model re-execution or use of speculative values and therefore does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: This test references receipt of a speculative event by the subscriber manager but contains no assertion that the subscriber manager re-executes a model or that speculative values are applied to a model run. It is topically related but provides no verification of the required behavior.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: This test verifies construction of a speculative query and arrival of a speculative event at the event server, but does not assert that the subscriber manager re-executes any model or that speculative values are used in such execution.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: This test describes event server behavior constructing composite messages for subscribers. It does not state that the subscriber manager re-executes a model with speculative values, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: TC147 concerns real-time publication of an event and subscriptions. It does not assert that the subscriber manager provides a report or determination about negative impacts on other requirements/constraints.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: TC149 pertains to non-speculative event notification and only asserts that the event notification message is stored in the local event log. There is no assertion that the subscriber manager reports whether a speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: TC150 covers receipt/processing of a speculative event notification but only states: "The event notification message is processed according to predefined protocols." It does not assert that the subscriber manager reports results indicating negative impacts on other requirements and constraints.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: TC151 describes user construction of a speculative query and that a speculative event message is received by the event server. It does not assert subscriber-manager reporting of negative-impact results.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: TC156 documents modify-event behavior and that existing links are changed to tentative status. It does not assert that the subscriber manager reports whether a speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: TC162 demonstrates that the event server collects related requirements' quantitative constraints and sends a customized composite message to subscribers. This provides related requirement data necessary to assess impact, but it does not assert that the subscriber manager evaluates/decides or reports whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints. Thus it partially supports the requirement (data preparation and delivery) but does not verify the reporting/verdict behavior.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC143 addresses subscriber registration with the subscriber manager. There is no mention of restoring executable model state or verifying prior-state consistency.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC144 tests subscriber failing to register and being removed. This concerns registration flow and removal, not restoration of an executable model or prior state verification.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC146 verifies registration of the subscriber manager with the event server. It does not verify any restore or prior-state restoration behavior.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC148 describes the subscriber manager coming online and downloading missed event notifications. This verifies retrieval of missed notifications, not restoration of an executable model to a prior state; there is no assertion about restoring model state, comparing pre/post state, or rollback.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC149 checks that incoming event notifications are received and stored in a local event log. This verifies event persistence, not restoration of an executable model to a prior runtime state.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC164 prevents processing events out of order. This is about ordering enforcement in processing, not about restoring the executable model to a previously saved state.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.
Reason: The test lists the trigger driver as a precondition and expects an event message to be sent, which maps to the requirement. As a high-level test description it lacks executable assertions or checks that the driver component triggered the message or handled failures.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.
Reason: The test explicitly requires the trigger driver to be running and expects a decompose event message to be sent, which aligns with the requirement's intent that a driver triggers events. However the artifact is a high-level test description (no executable assertions shown), and it does not exercise error conditions, driver invocation details, or verify the driver API/interaction.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.
Reason: Precondition and post-condition state the driver is running and a refine event message is sent, demonstrating intended use of a trigger driver. Missing are concrete assertions that the driver component was invoked, error/negative cases, and low-level verification of the driver behavior.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.

Description: RQ121 Event types shall include adding new requirements.

Description: RQ124 Event types shall include modifying a requirement.

Description: RQ124 Event types shall include modifying a requirement.
Reason: This test covers a refine event (adding a related requirement) rather than a modify event; it does not assert the modify event behavior required.

Description: RQ124 Event types shall include modifying a requirement.

Description: RQ124 Event types shall include modifying a requirement.

Description: RQ125 Event types shall include deleting a requirement.
Reason: This test exercises a 'decompose event' and asserts decomposition behaviors; it does not assert deletion behavior or a delete event.

Description: RQ125 Event types shall include deleting a requirement.
Reason: This test describes a 'refine event' and asserts refinement behaviors; it does not specify or assert any delete event or deletion-related outcomes.

Description: RQ125 Event types shall include deleting a requirement.
Reason: This test describes a 'modify event' and its post-conditions; it does not reference or verify a delete event.

Description: RQ125 Event types shall include deleting a requirement.

Description: RQ126 Event types shall include merging requirements.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: This test describes a requirement change causing an event notification to be sent (shows the trigger side). It does not assert that the eventserver receives messages originating from requirement changes.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.

Description: RQ130 The eventserver shall differentiate between evolutionary and speculative events.

Description: RQ130 The eventserver shall differentiate between evolutionary and speculative events.

Description: RQ131 When an evolutionary event occurs; the eventserver shall notify subscribers of the event.

Description: RQ131 When an evolutionary event occurs; the eventserver shall notify subscribers of the event.

Description: RQ131 When an evolutionary event occurs; the eventserver shall notify subscribers of the event.

Description: RQ133 The eventserver shall identify all related requirements and constraints for each impacted executable model.

It does not, however, explicitly assert delivery to 'each impacted executable model' (multiplicity across all impacted executable models), nor does it assert acknowledgements, retries, or mapping from subscriber to an executable model endpoint—hence partial confidence.

Description: RQ136 The eventserver shall send the customized message to each impacted executable model.

Description: RQ137 The user shall view all evolutionary events received for a specific artifact in the order in which they were received.

Description: RQ137 The user shall view all evolutionary events received for a specific artifact in the order in which they were received.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.
Reason: TC162 focuses on the event server constructing composite event messages and sending them; it does not assert that a user updates related artifacts using those messages.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC164 describes preventing out-of-order processing of events and that no events are processed when attempted out-of-order. It does not assert removal of events as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces/tentative links.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC165 explicitly states that when the user discards tentative links (i.e., eliminates temporary traces), any additional event messages that were sent because of those tentative links and for which the tentative link has been eliminated are removed from the event processing list and display. This is a direct match to the requirement's mandate to automatically eliminate events that are no longer valid due to user elimination of temporary traces.
